title: Inaccurate AVG of SQRT with 51M records
poc: insert into biglist2 select rt from biglist limit 10000000;
insert into biglist2 select rt from biglist limit 10;
select avg(sqrt(rt)) as avg from biglist2;
insert into biglist2 select rt from biglist2;
select 'count(n1) == 10',        count(*), 'avg(n1) == 1.21', avg(a), 'avg(sqrt(n1)) == 1.1', avg(sqrt(a)) from n1;
select 'count(d1) == 10',        count(*), 'avg(d1) == 1.21', avg(a), 'avg(sqrt(d1)) == 1.1', avg(sqrt(a)) from d1;
select 'count(n8) == 100000000', count(*), 'avg(n8) == 1.21', avg(a), 'avg(sqrt(n8)) == 1.1', avg(sqrt(a)) from n8;
select 'count(d8) == 100000000', count(*), 'avg(d8) == 1.21', avg(a), 'avg(sqrt(d8)) == 1.1', avg(sqrt(a)) from d8;
insert into big_s select * from biglist2;
select avg(rt), min(rt), max(rt) from big_s;
select avg(rt), avg(sqrt(rt)), min(rt), max(rt) from big_s;
insert into big_d select * from biglist2;
select avg(rt), min(rt), max(rt) from big_d;
select avg(rt), avg(sqrt(rt)), min(rt), max(rt) from big_d;
key_bug_features: Inaccurate AVG calculation for SQRT with large datasets
bug_features_seed: INSERT INTO large_data SELECT ROUND(RANDOM() * 100, 2) FROM generate_series(1, 51000000);
SELECT AVG(SQRT(value)) FROM large_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
INSERT INTO temp_data SELECT SQRT(value) FROM large_data WHERE value > 0;
